channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Numerous Potholes, 16th April
item.description:
As previously reported these potholes in Salamanca Road, back last year and your email on 5th Dec 25 you would look into it, nothing done so I reported again as you will see from the records, your workers have driven up the road this morning and sprayed paint around all the potholes on the lower half of the street but from the corner to no 30 not one drop of white paint and they are as bad as the ones lower down can you explain why, is it because it us higher nujmbers that have reported it and you are making us suffer even more and just attending to the lower house numbers problems an explanation please would be great
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 14, Kimberley Walk, Oakley, Cheltenham
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: At busy junction of Promenade/Imperial sqiare, 16th April
item.description:
Forces vehicles to move into adjoining lane, accident risk
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 101, Promenade, Montpellier, Cheltenham
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: TMC - Defects -CW6 Potholes problem, 16th April
item.description:

Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: TMC - Defects -CW6 Potholes problem, 16th April

item.title: Pothole at edge of busy road, 16th April
item.description:
Previously patched depression has opened again and is becoming deeper and larger with every rainfall
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 36, Park Place, Warden Hill, Cheltenham
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Light sensor is not detecting vehicles problem, 16th April
item.description:
Wales & West works. Temp lights stuck on red. Lots of issues as there are 3 schools in the area. Lots of traffic congestion as a result.
Report on FixMyStreet
item.category: Light sensor is not detecting vehicles

item.title: Overflowing bin, 16th April
item.description:
Bin overflowing, rubbish surrounding base
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Barnett Way, Barnwood, Gloucester
Report on FixMyStreet
